experiments.statusDetail: Parser.Always builds clean post-investigation; see swift-parser-primitives production code (Phase 1b stale-triage 2026-04-30)
experiments.supersededBy: Parser.Always builds clean post-investigation; see swift-parser-primitives production code

experiments.purpose: Compare throughput: pointfreeco/swift-parsing vs parser-primitives pattern

Empirically test whether `Parser.`Protocol`: ~Copyable` on Self introduces a cross-module witness-table SIGSEGV under current toolchains, per the 2026-02-14 Research/witness-table-sigsegv-with-noncopyable-protocol-constraints.md crash pattern (Swift 6.2.3, swiftlang/swift#85441 pattern). Six variants: V1-V2 leaf Fail with local Input; V3-V4 composed Map<Fail<...>> single + triple-nested; V5 full original-crash conditions (external-package Input.Slice<Buffer<UInt8>.Linear>, composed, cross-module, Self: ~Copyable); V6 Parser.Lazy closure-returning-~Copyable. Toolchains: Swift 6.2.3 / 6.3.2 (default) / 6.4-dev nightly 2026-05-07.
experiments.statusDetail: Hypothesis `Self: ~Copyable on Parser.\`Protocol\` triggers a witness-table SIGSEGV` REFUTED. All variants PASS on Swift 6.3.2 with the full original-crash conditions present in V5. The 2026-02-14 documented crash on Swift 6.2.3 appears to have been resolved by an unannounced Swift 6.3.x compiler fix. Authored in support of swift-institute/Research/2026-05-13-parser-protocol-noncopyable-escapable-relaxation.md v1.1.0 — downgrades the witness-table SIGSEGV cost item from `verified-unfixed compiler bug` (v1.0.0 dominant) to `resolved-empirically` (v1.1.0).
experiments.statusRaw: REFUTED (SIGSEGV does not reproduce on Swift 6.3.2)
